items.title: Crowds flock to Antananarivo as Madagascar restores Its pre‑colonial new year
items.description: Madagascar's traditional New Year, Alahamady Be, a pre-colonial custom abandoned for decades, has now been revived with rituals honouring ancestors, sacred fire and purification ceremonies.

items.title: 'Trump could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment,' ex-adviser John Bolton says
items.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, former US national security adviser John Bolton said US President Donald Trump "could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment". Bolton also said he remains mystified by Trump's reversal on regime change in Iran and warned that the Iranian opposition is not receiving enough support.

items.title: Madagascar’s president orders polygraph for aspiring ministers
items.description: Madagascar's military leader, President Michael Randrianirina, announced Thursday that anyone applying to become a minister in the new government will undergo lie detector tests to screen for corruption—a rare and unprecedented vetting measure.

items.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
items.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

items.title: Europe's housing crisis: Is the EU plan up to the job?
items.description: MEPs recently approved a key report on housing, with the stated aim of securing decent, sustainable and affordable housing. They are responding to an EU-wide crisis in which property prices jumped by 15.5 percent between 2021 and 2024, according to Eurostat. That has frozen out millions of people looking to get onto the property ladder. Rental prices have continued to go up as well. Housing is a competence of member states, but there has been a push to do more at the EU level. We debate just how much Brussels should get involved, and whether the solutions it is proposing are workable.

items.title: 'No obvious short-term way out' of war, French top diplomat tells Israel
items.description: Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said on Friday he did not see an obvious end to ​the conflict in the Middle East in the short-term, but that France and its allies would continue to work towards trying to find a lasting solution. "There is no obvious short-term ​way out ‌of the ongoing regional escalation, which has in some ways been ⁠unfolding since October 7, 2023. But this must in no way serve as a pretext for inaction," Barrot told reporters ‌after meeting with Israeli counterpart Gideon Saar in Tel Aviv.

items.title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig
items.description: K-pop megastars BTS released a new album Friday ahead of their open-air comeback concert in Seoul, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people. It will be BTS's first concert after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service.

items.title: Man convicted of newborn's manslaughter had assaulted infant years earlier
items.description: Robert Alan Gee, who has been jailed for the manslaughter of baby Michael Willmott, had in 2003 assaulted a 3-month-old girl in a different state.

items.title: Morning news brief
items.description: Global oil and energy prices reel as the war in the Middle East escalates, Trump talks about potential for ground troops in Iran and the possibility of seizing Kharg Island.

items.title: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto calls acid attack on activist Andrie Yunus 'terrorism'
items.description: "This is terrorism, isn't it? A barbaric act. We must pursue (the perpetrators)," Prabowo Subianto said in an interview

items.title: Epworth releases details of hospital review after surgeon allegations
items.description: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

items.title: BTS return from military service
items.description: The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days.
items.source: but does their album live up to the hype?

items.title: Why the UK Labour party's Brexit focus has shifted from Leavers to Remainers
items.description: Will the pursuit of a closer relationship with the EU risk courting electoral disaster by alienating Brexit-backing voters?

items.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians caught in the crossfire of Middle East war
items.description: Four women were killed in the occupied #WestBank following missile fire from #Iran - the first Palestinian victims of the Middle East #war since it started on February 28. For Palestinians, it is yet another conflict they find themselves caught up in, with little protection.

items.title: NGOs in DR Congo offer street children skills and hope of a better future
items.description: Several thousand children are estimated to live on the streets in the Congolese capital, Kinshasa.

items.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
items.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

items.title: Cuba ‘categorically’ rejects prospect of removing Diaz-Canel in US talks
items.description: Minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio says he is open to US trade talks but refused to entertain a leadership shake-up.

items.title: Watch: EU announces €450 million in humanitarian aid to Gulf countries hit by West Asia war
Drone defense in the Iran war: What can Ukraine offer?
items.description: As the Iran war continues apace, Ukraine's hard-won expertise in countering drone swarms is sparking interest around the world.

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates on March 19, 2026: Premium petrol prices hiked by 2-3/litre, says Oil Ministry
items.description: The Pentagon is seeking $200 billion in additional funds for the Iran war, a senior administration official says

items.title: Who are the Gulf’s military allies, and how are they helping in Iran war?
items.description: Experts say while Gulf states need support in face of Iranian attacks, it is less clear what their allies can provide.

items.title: Iran developing a ‘vetting system’ for Strait of Hormuz transit: Report
items.description: Lloyd's List reports that ships could be allowed to pass through 'safe corridor' once they are approved by IRGC.

items.title: Burkina Faso’s tomato export ban raises concerns over regional supply
items.description: Burkina Faso’s decision to ban the export of fresh tomatoes is drawing attention across West Africa, with traders and consumers watching closely for possible effects on supply and prices.

items.title: Germany: Police dismantle major cyberattack 'botnets'
items.description: German authorities have shut down two of the world’s largest "botnets" as part of a joint three-nation operation. Each of the networks hijacked millions of compromised everyday devices to take part in cyberattacks.

items.title: A cultural stroll through Paris with singer Yael Naim and comedian Philippine Delaire
items.description: This week Valérie Fayolle sits down with Yael Naim, who takes us round the Gaîté Lyrique, at the beating heart of the Paris cultural scene. She tells us about her seventh album, "Solaire", an ode to freedom, imperfection and femininity. Next up is vivacious Philippine Delaire, treading the boards of the Comédie de Paris. The actress and comedian is the star of "Daddy's Girl".

items.title: Amazonia's Indigenous peoples dismantle Western cliches
items.description: Indigenous peoples and the Amazon rainsforest have often been exoticized. A new exhibition challenges these long-held tropes.

items.title: Teenagers sue Musk’s xAI claiming image-generator made sexually explicit images of them as minors
items.description: Other AI companies have prohibited their image-generators from producing any sexually explicit content, even of adults. Mr. Musk saw this as a business opportunity and promoted the ability of xAI's Grok chatbot to create “spicy” content, the lawsuit claims.

items.title: Nowruz in Tehran: Crowded markets dispte ongoing airstrikes
items.description: Iran’s capital Tehran showed signs of cautious activity on 19 March 2026 as residents prepared for Nowruz, despite ongoing US-Israeli strikes that have disrupted daily life since 28 February.

items.title: Iran's Khamenei says enemy 'defeated' in Nowruz message
items.description: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Friday said the enemies of the Islamic republic were being defeated in the war against the US and Israel in a written message for the Persian New Year, Nowruz. The message was read out on Iranian television, with Khamenei yet to appear publicly since the beginning of the war.

items.title: ‘Cowards’: Trump slams NATO over lack of support in US–Israel war on Iran
items.description: Trump is calling for US allies to help guarantee shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as NATO relocates Iraq personnel.

items.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
items.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.

items.title: Air raid sirens blare during Eid call to prayer in Kuwait
items.description: Air raid sirens sounded across Kuwait City as Iran targeted US assets with missiles and drones.

items.title: Niger rejects EU's call to free ousted President Bazoum
items.description: The junta government has rebuffed a European Parliament resolution demanding the release of ex-President Bazoum. It further warned the EU to stop meddling in Niger's internal affairs.

items.title: Iran video said to show US F-35 being hit
items.description: Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news agency has released military video said to show air defence systems hitting a US F-35.

items.title: Paraguay, underdog Trump ally in Latin America, becomes an investor magnet
items.description: Entrepreneurs flock to Paraguay from across the region as sleek towers rise above the once-sleepy capital and its conservative president cozies up to the US. Leer más

items.title: Adorni under pressure as fresh complaint questions state contracts
items.description: New complaint from lawmaker alleges irregularities in public tenders linked to Tecnópolis exhibition centre and venue, adding to scrutiny over the Cabinet chief’s conduct and potential conflicts of interest involving his wife. Leer más

items.title: Could Iran war trigger the next global food shock?
items.description: From factories to supermarket shelves, the Iran war is disrupting global supply chains.

Liverpool condemns 'dehumanising, cowardly' racist abuse of Ibrahima Konate
items.description: Liverpool defender Ibrahima Konate was the target of “vile and abhorrent” racist abuse online, the Premier League club said Friday.

items.title: Man found guilty of killing passenger during drug-fuelled road trip
items.description: A Supreme Court jury delivers a verdict in the case of two men charged over a string of public shootings on the NSW mid north coast in 2023.

items.title: Trump calls NATO ‘cowards’ over lack of support in Iran war
items.description: The U.S. President complained NATO countries did not want ⁠to join the fight against Iran, yet still complain about high ‌oil prices

items.title: Tunisian anti-racism activist sentenced to eight years in prison
items.description: A Tunisian court on Thursday sentenced anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah to eight years in prison on financial misconduct charges, a ruling her lawyer condemned as "shocking" and politically motivated, marking the latest crackdown on civil society in the North African country.

items.title: Czechs investigate fire at warehouse after reports of anti-Israel group claiming responsibility
items.description: The company had previously said it was planning to open a center to develop and produce drones and train personnel in cooperation with Israeli Elbit Systems, a military technology company

items.title: Bola Tinubu's state visit to UK: major steel deal agreed with British PM
items.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer welcomed Nigerian President Bola Tinubu at 10 Downing Street in London during the first state visit by the leader of Africa's most populous nation in nearly four decades.

items.title: Colombia: Torrential rain triggers severe flooding in Facatativá, over 1,000 affected
items.description: Torrential rain that began on 18 March triggered severe flooding in Facatativá, a town 40 km west of Bogotá, affecting more than 1,000 families, according to local authorities.

items.title: Katherine braces for second major flood in two weeks as cyclone approaches NT
items.description: Just two weeks after its worst flood in almost 30 years, Katherine businesses are being sandbagged and a field hospital has been set up as locals brace for another major weather event.

items.title: Trump administration sues Harvard over treatment of Jewish and Israeli students, seeks billions of dollars
items.description: Harvard will defend against the lawsuit, which a spokesperson called “yet another ‌pretextual ⁠and retaliatory action by the administration for refusing to turn over control of Harvard to the federal ⁠government”

items.title: Bachelorette's new season pulled after Taylor Frankie Paul abuse allegations
items.description: The decision comes after footage emerged appearing to show the reality star in an altercation with her ex-partner.

items.title: South Africa's traditional circumcision claims dozens of young lives
items.description: At least 48 boys and young men died during recent initiation ceremonies in South Africa, among them 22-year-old Lamkelo Mtyho, who had no known health issues when he entered a registered school—but never returned.

items.title: Iranian TV shows man rescued from South Khorasan rubble
items.description: Red Crescent teams rescue survivors in South Khorasan after reported US-Israeli strikes, as Iran counts rising civilian toll and mounting destruction.

items.title: BTS: Everything you need to know about the K-pop comeback of the year
items.description: After a four-year hiatus and the end of their military service, BTS are returning as a full group. With a new album, a Netflix livestream and a major world tour on the horizon, the K-pop icons are entering a new chapter.

items.title: Refugee Council questions Australia’s Iran strategy at Women’s Asian Cup
items.description: Women's Asian Cup hosts Australia face criticism for the political tug of war over the Iranian women's football team.

items.title: Watch: Trump’s Pearl Harbor joke tests US-Japan alliance amid Iran tensions
items.description: Donald Trump joked about Pearl Harbor while urging Sanae Takaichi to expand Japan’s role in securing the Strait of Hormuz, drawing a muted response.

items.title: Indian killed in Saudi Arabia after Iranian missile debris hit
items.description: War’s second Indian fatality in Saudi Arabia after one person who died on March 8; Indian embassy in Riyadh advises 26 lakh Indians in the kingdom to ‘stay vigilant, strictly adhere to safety guidelines’

items.title: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei says Iran dealt enemies 'dizzying blow'
items.description: Israel said it had confirmed that Ismaeil Ahmadi, the chief of Iran's Basij paramilitary force, was "eliminated" in a strike earlier this week that also killed its top commander

items.title: Salman Rushdie on why tyrants fear artists
items.description: As the renowned writer tours Germany to discuss his latest book, "The Eleventh Hour," he explains why he chooses to remain optimistic, despite all of the world's reasons to despair.

items.title: Iran’s Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in written Nowruz message
items.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since he replaced his slain father as Iran's supreme leader.

items.title: Work from home, avoid air travel to deal with higher energy prices: International Energy Agency
items.description: The U.S-Israel war ‌with Iran has driven up energy prices, causing concerns over inflation ‌across the world

items.title: Iran war: Trump lashes out at NATO over lack of Hormuz help
items.description: Trump called NATO allies "cowards" after multiple European officials ruled out sending naval forces to secure the Strait of Hormuz. Israel said it was continuing to strike "infrastructure" in Tehran. DW has more.

items.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
items.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

items.title: Alliance of Sahel states outraged at EU demand to free Niger's president
items.description: The European parliament voted last week to pass a resolution demanding the "immediate" release of Niger's former president Mohamed Bazoum, who was ousted in a coup in 2023 and has been detained ever since. The Alliance of Sahel states reacted with indignation.

items.title: Norway's crown princess says she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department's release of ⁠millions of Epstein documents has sent shockwaves around the world, revealing the disgraced financier's ties to prominent people, including the crown ‌princess and top Norwegian politicians, business executives and diplomats

items.title: 'Cowards': Trump lashes out at NATO over Strait of Hormuz as oil prices spike
items.description: The US president claims it would be "easy" for members of the western alliance to protect the shipping route, amid reports America plans to blockade Iran's strategically-important Kharg Island.

items.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
items.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

items.title: What is France hoping to achieve with FM's Middle East visit?
items.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot visited Israel Friday after a trip to Lebanon's Beirut the day before. It is part of a push by France for Israel to hold direct talks with the Lebanese government in what would be an historic first. But what is France hoping to achieve with the talks? France 24's James Andre takes a look.

items.title: ByteDance to sell gaming unit Moonton to Saudi PIF-owned firm
Four in ten workers in Argentina are informally employed, UBA report finds
items.description: UBA report says that seven in ten workers aged between 16 and 24 are in informal employment, reflecting the difficulties facing this labour group. Leer más

items.title: Slovenia election: a battle for the soul of the country?
items.description: Sunday's parliamentary election in Slovenia is shaping up to be a tight race between the governing center-left GS and the right-wing SDS. Nevertheless, smaller parties are likely to hold the key to forming a government.

items.title: Abu Dhabi police arrest over 100 for filming, posting 'misleading' information during war
items.description: Similar measures have been taken across the Gulf as Iran hits U.S. assets but also civilian infrastructure including landmarks, oil and gas facilities, airports and residential areas

items.title: Iran war 'worst' time for tax, say gas companies
items.description: The Greens and One Nation are among those that want gas profits levied, with pressure mounting on Labor to respond to growing calls for reform.

items.title: Trump administration sues Harvard over alleged antisemitism
items.description: The Justice Department has accused Harvard of being "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students, and intentionally refusing to enforce its campus rules when victims are Jews or Israelis.

items.description: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian told a media briefing that envoys are an important bridge for fostering friendly and cooperative relations between nations

items.title: Cameroon delays parliamentary elections, lawmakers to stay until end 2026
items.description: Cameroonian lawmakers yet again voted on Thursday to extend their mandate, according to official media.

items.title: As Cuba struggles with power cuts, how is the island holding up?
items.description: The BBC's Will Grant reports from the island days after the entire national electric grid collapsed.

items.title: Iran: From Khamenei to Khamenei
items.description: How Iran’s power structure was built, and how it survives its architect.

items.title: Fuel crisis a major political test for the Albanese government
items.description: Scott Morrison's assertion that the chase to secure vaccines in early 2021 was "not a race" cost him dearly. A similar global contest is already underway, only this time it involves securing energy supplies.

items.title: War in Iran threatens fresh food-price shock across developing world
K-Pop stars BTS release new album ahead of comeback concert
items.description: The BTS comeback concert, to be held at Seoul's historic Gwanghwamun Square, is expected to draw around 260,000 people. The event will be livestreamed by Netflix in around 190 countries.

items.title: Iran women’s soccer team greeted with a welcome ceremony in Tehran
items.description: Members of Iran’s national women’s soccer team were greeted with a welcome ceremony upon their return to the Islamic Republic after several of the players sought asylum in Australia

items.title: Greig Fraser filmed the unfilmable for Dune. Now he's back for Project Hail Mary
items.description: With most of Project Hail Mary's action taking place in far off corners of space it was up to Australian cinematographer Greig Fraser to create what we've never seen.

items.title: Ready-to-wear autumn-winter 2026/27 collections: Is fashion still subversive?
items.description: Is fashion still subversive? The answer is yes, with the extraordinarily named Matières Fécales label. At Yohji Yamamoto, it's yes, but more delicately. Satoshi Kondo for Issey Miyake, meanwhile, advocates for movement without constraints. Historian-performer Olivier Saillard elevates everyday clothing with his Living Fashion Museum. Lilia Litkovska, from Ukraine, draws inspiration from her war-torn country. And Ludovic Winterstan, for La Fabrique Nomade, believes it's high time we accepted each other, wherever our country of origin.

items.title: No, Iran didn't publish a 'list' of 11 American cities to target
items.description: A video claiming that Iran published a list of 11 American cities it plans to target has gone viral since it was first posted on Instagram on March 3. We found the origin of this list, and it turns out that it has nothing to do with Iran.

items.title: India news: Indian national killed in Riyadh strike
items.description: India's embassy in Riyadh said an Indian national was killed on March 18, bringing the total number of Indian victims in the Middle East to six.

items.title: Explosion hits Jerusalem’s Old City after Iran missile alert
items.description: An explosion struck Jerusalem’s Old City seconds after sirens warned of an incoming Iranian missile.

items.title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Gulf states come under drone, missile attacks
items.description: Iran has stepped up attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure in recent days, hitting refineries and the world’s biggest gas hub in Qatar, as Tehran retaliated over Israeli strikes on its South Pars gas field

items.title: Iranians celebrate Persian New Year in first wartime Nowruz in decades
items.description: Residents of Tehran exchange new year's wishes as US and Israeli attacks continue through the spring equinox.

items.title: UK–Nigeria relations in focus as London hosts Tinubu
items.description: Britain has rolled out the red carpet for Nigerian President Bola Tinubu, marking the first state visit by a Nigerian leader in 37 years. Amid severe geopolitical quakes, what do both countries aim to achieve?

items.title: Japan's PM walks tightrope in high-stakes Washington visit
items.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi travelled to Washington hoping to strengthen ties with the United States. The visit turned into a delicate balancing act amid US President Donald Trump's growing pressure on allies to help the US in the Iran war. Yuka Royer discusses the visit with James Schoff, Senior Director at the Sasakawa Peace Foundation USA.

items.title: “We are living in fear”: Gay people in Senegal amid crackdown
items.description: Senegal is among more than half of African countries where same-sex relations are illegal. But it is now moving toward stricter enforcement.

items.title: Flights, petrol cars and cruise ships: Amsterdam bans fossil fuel ads
items.description: Cities around the world are clearing their billboards of ads for flights, cruise ships and petrol cars in an effort to reduce planet-warming gases. Amsterdam is the latest city to join the movement, becoming the first capital in the world to approve a legal ban on fossil fuel advertising. Dutch activists are now calling for restrictions to be enforced on a national level. Our Down to Earth team reports.

items.title: 🔴 Live: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant statement on Persian New Year as blasts rock Tehran
Child sex abuse survivor 'vindicated' despite perpetrator walking free
items.description: A member of Melbourne's ultra-orthodox Jewish community now living in Israel says he feels vindicated and free despite his abuser walking free from court and avoiding more jail time.

items.title: Bulldog Baker's brilliant goal seals thrilling win over fast-finishing Crows
items.description: The Western Bulldogs hang on to claim a thrilling six-point win over Adelaide, with a brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinching the win as the Crows stormed home.

items.title: What US-Israeli targets reveal about Iran war goals three weeks in
items.description: From degradation of military to efforts to foment unrest, targets show array of objectives but no single endgame.

items.title: Norway's Crown Princess says she was 'deceived' by Epstein
items.description: Norway's Crown Princess has told national TV that she had been "manipulated and deceived" by Jeffrey Epstein and wished she had never met him.

items.title: Finding an escape from modern life in the booming world of arts and crafts
items.description: Craft trends come and go, but a resurgence in social arts and crafts is proving that creativity and human connection are still very much in vogue.

items.title: Iran threatens to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide
items.description: Iran threatened to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide and insisted it was still building missiles

items.title: Fire at South Korean auto parts factory injures at least 53
items.description: Some people were injured while jumping from the building during escape attempts, while others suffered from smoke inhalation, the fire chief of the city’s Daedeok district said. Police were tracking mobile phone signals of the 14 people still unaccounted for.

items.title: Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
items.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that even overwhelming force cannot guarantee safety when asymmetric tools, such as drones and decentralised command structures, remain effective. At its core, he contends that reopening the Strait is not a technical or logistical challenge, but a political and diplomatic one.

items.title: Ecolab to buy CoolIT for $4.75 billion to tap into AI data center boom
Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
items.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

items.title: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it
items.description: The real risk lies not in closure, but in disruption in the strait’s approaches.

items.title: 'Polish populists have decided to turn anti-European': Warsaw mayor Rafal Trzaskowski
items.description: Talking Europe travels to Warsaw to meet the mayor of the city and former presidential candidate Rafal Trzaskowski, just as the political temperature rises ahead of high-stakes parliamentary elections scheduled for next year. Trzaskowski argues that a long-standing consensus in Polish politics – being pro-European and pro-Ukraine – has been broken, as populist forces try to outdo each other in an effort to attract voters. Trzaskowski is an ally of Prime Minister Donald Tusk and was Civic Coalition's candidate in the June 2025 presidential election. He was elected mayor of Warsaw in October 2018.

items.title: Paramedic care 'below reasonable standard' in death in custody, coroner finds
items.description: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

items.title: Russia, China and the US – the global winners and losers of the Iran war
items.description: As the conflict triggers economic disruption some risk being hit hard, while others stand to benefit.

items.title: Exiled Iranian writer Shahrnush Parsipur on her International Booker Prize-nominated novella
items.description: Published in 1989, the book was seen as an act of insurrection and Parsipur was jailed, while the novella was banned

items.title: Several injured as turbulence hits Delta flight into Sydney
items.description: Three flight attendants were taken to hospital after the Airbus A350 experienced turbulence during its descent into Sydney Airport.

items.title: Nuclear's cleanup cost threatens the expansion dream
items.description: Countries are racing to build new reactors. But we've barely figured out how to clean up the old ones — and the bill is potentially staggering.

items.title: Former childcare worker jailed for indecent treatment of child
items.description: A former childcare worker from Fiji is sentenced to jail for the indecent treatment of a child at a daycare centre in Queensland, and will be deported afterwards.

items.title: Myth of liberation: As war rages, trapped Iranians 'curse' the regime, the Americans & the Israelis
items.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Borzou Daragahi, award-winning international correspondent and investigative reporter. As Iran marks Nowruz, the Persian New Year, war intrudes into civilian spaces. The psychological terrain is marked less by overt anger than by fatigue, resignation, and a deepening sense of entrapment. A media blackout and communication breakdowns intensify this isolation, eroding social cohesion and amplifying uncertainty. External narratives of “liberation” fail to resonate with civilians who find themselves in survival mode.

items.title: UK and Nigeria agree deal to speed up deportations
items.description: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have signed a new agreement aimed at accelerating the return of Nigerians without legal status in Britain, including failed asylum seekers, visa overstayers and convicted offenders.

items.title: Action movie star Chuck Norris has died aged 86
items.description: Actor, martial artist and the source of a meme about him being unbeatable, has passed away.

items.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
items.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

items.title: Social media makes people unhappy — World Happiness Report
items.description: The 2026 World Happiness Report found that life satisfaction among those under 25 in some English-speaking countries has fallen sharply over the past decade. Finland was once again the happiest country.

items.title: Slovenia heads to polls with diverging views on Israel in focus
items.description: Slovenia's election sees the centre-left coalition take on the populist right amid claims of foreign meddling.

items.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas a formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
items.description: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.

items.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
items.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

items.title: More than 1,600 new gas wells approved for rural Queensland until 2081
items.description: A major multinational gas venture has just secured federal approval to ramp up extraction in rural Queensland — locking in operations for the next 55 years.

items.title: Palestinians celebrate Eid in Gaza, making the most of a fragile ceasefire
items.description: The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.

items.title: Chuck Norris, film star and martial artist, dies aged 86
items.description: Norris' epic fight with kung-fu superstar Bruce Lee in The Way of The Dragon helped him Norris into an icon, and he went on to star in more than 20 movies.

items.title: French jihadist sentenced to life in jail over IS group genocide of Iraq's Yazidis
items.description: A court in France has sentenced jihadist Sabri Essid for his part in "the genocide perpetrated by the Islamic State group" against Iraq's Yazidi minority, in the first French trial of its kind. Essid is presumed to have been killed in 2018, but without proof of his death he was tried and convicted in absentia.

items.title: Argentina wins discovery stay in US$16-billion YPF US court case
items.description: Government wins order blocking holders of a US$16.1-billion US judgment against it in YPF nationalisation case. Leer más

items.title: Iran today, Africa tomorrow
items.description: What is being done to Iran today could be done to Africa tomorrow. Africa must reject this illegal war.

items.title: Egyptians attend prayers at a mosque to mark Eid celebrations
items.description: Egyptians attended prayers at a mosque in Cairo on Friday to mark the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, and bid farewell to the to the Islamic holy month of Ramadan.

items.title: Dana Lixenberg's personal, political and pared-down portraits
items.description: Her photographs of celebrated personalities, from Toni Morrison to Tupac Shakur, have become iconic images. Dana Lixenberg tells us about three decades of providing her unique perspective on the United States and the evolution of a society she first started documenting in the 1990s. As her major, monographic exhibition "American Images" opens at the Maison Européenne de la Photo in Paris, the Dutch photographer tells us about being surprised by the enduring success of her beautiful black-and-white image of Tupac, how a small community in South Central LA crystallised her love of image-making and why artifice in photography is all right in very small doses.

items.title: 'Transitional moment': Leaders make final pitches ahead of SA election, One Nation leading Liberals in latest poll
items.description: The major parties have made their final campaign pitches to South Australian voters as a Newspoll survey puts primary support for One Nation at 22 per cent, ahead of the Liberals on just 16 per cent. One political analyst says it is a "transitional moment in South Australian politics".

items.title: Trump compares attack on Iran to Pearl Harbor in meeting with Japanese PM
items.description: In a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister, US President Donald Trump was asked why he didn't inform allies about his plan to attack Iran.

items.description: Six major international powers, including Britain, France, Germany and Japan, said on Thursday they were ready “to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz”.

items.title: Court in Buenos Aires orders blocking of Polymarket in Argentina

items.title: Gulf crisis draws attention to African oil [Business Africa]
items.description: Unlike crude from the middle east, a lot of African oil does not have to transit vulnerable corridors such as the Strait of Hormuz, where events like war can put a stop to shipping. But years of constrained investment meant African producers cannot pump more and fast enough to cover supply gaps

items.title: A look at the economic cost of the war in the Middle East
items.description: The team from NPR's The Indicator podcast takes a look at the economic costs of the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Poland to recognize same-sex marriages from EU states
items.description: Poland's top administrative court has ruled that same-sex marriages performed in other EU countries must be recognized. The decision follows a Polish couple who married in Germany but were denied registration in Warsaw.

items.title: More Marines are headed to Middle East as Iran war reaches the 3-week mark
items.description: The USS Boxer group of three ships, carrying thousands of Marines from the 11the Marine Expeditionary Unit, has left California and will reach the Persian Gulf in about three weeks, NPR has confirmed.

items.description: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.

items.title: Iran war's shockwaves impact Turkish tourism industry
items.description: Turkey is one of the most popular vacation spots in the world. But in the wake of the Iran war, there's been a flurry of cancellations. And it's not just tourists from Iran that are staying away.

items.description: The Senegalese Football Federation (FSF) said Thursday that the Africa Cup title cannot be taken away from Senegal, following a decision by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) to strip the country of its title earlier this week.

items.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he believes pipelines should be built to ‌transport Middle East oil and gas across the Arabian Peninsula and up to Israeli ports to avoid threats by Iran in the Hormuz Strait and other Gulf waters.

items.title: The people remodelling homes with reclaimed ruins
items.description: Cities are quietly becoming raw‑material hubs as urban miners turn rubble into a carbon‑saving construction supply chain.

items.title: News24 | Switzerland blocks weapons exports to US over Iran war
items.description: Switzerland has said it will not issue licences for companies to export weapons to the United States due to the ongoing war on Iran, citing the country’s longstanding principle of neutrality.

items.description: US Chief Justice John Roberts said that hostility directed in personal terms at judges is “dangerous and it’s got to stop”, commenting just days after US President Donald Trump’s latest social media broadside against judges ‌who have ruled against him and his administration.

items.title: Eid al-Fitr celebrations begin under shadow of war
items.description: Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of the holy Muslim month of Ramadan, began this Friday in countries across the globe. But in many parts of the world, the celebrations are being overshadowed by war.

items.title: News24 | ‘We’re boiling water for drinking’: Stranded Gulf seafarers ration food and water
items.description: Seafarers stuck in the Gulf say they are rationing food and water and anxiously hoping supplies will get through Iran’s blockade in the war that has stranded them for three weeks.

items.title: Vietnam accelerates shift to ethanol fuel as Iran war drives up energy prices
items.description: Fuel prices in Vietnam have surged since the U.S.-Israel war on Iran began three weeks ago, ‌with gasoline prices up ​50% and diesel prices rising 70%, according to data from ⁠top fuel trader Petrolimex

items.title: Nasa's Artemis Moon rocket rolls back to pad for possible April launch
items.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

items.description: Milei's government confirms departure after one-year notice period; Foreign Minister Quirno insists country will remain engaged in global health efforts through regional and bilateral channels. Leer más

items.title: Teen jailed for 23 years over murder of seven-year-old in carjacking
items.description: Court in La Plata hands down 23-year sentence for the attack in which the minor was dragged for 15 blocks; Attacker was 17 at the time of the fatal carjacking. Leer más

items.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
items.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

items.title: Three men charged with conspiring to smuggle U.S. artificial intelligence to China
items.description: The men violated U.S. export controls laws by scheming to divert massive quantities of the high-performance servers assembled in the United States to China between 2024 and 2025

items.title: Iran war triggers helium shortage, hits semiconductor supply
items.description: Rising tension in the Middle East is rattling high-end chipmakers. Among the latest geopolitical uncertainties for the industry is a shortage of helium that could slow global production.

items.title: Iran war: Assassinations leave little room for negotiation
items.description: With most of its leaders killed, those stepping up to take their place in the Iranian regime appear to be far less willing to compromise. Is Washington's strategy of decapitation strikes backfiring?

items.title: US easing ban on Iranian oil would signal ‘beginning of the end of war’
items.description: The US says it is considering removing sanctions on Iranian oil stranded on tankers at sea.

items.title: 'Asking why honey is better than s***': Japan coach spins favouritism ahead of Matildas clash
items.description: Japan's coach says determining a favourite for the Women's Asian Cup final between his team and Australia is a matter for bees and flies.

items.title: Argentina to face Guatemala friend in place of scrapped Finalissima
items.description: World champions will play Guatemala in a friendly – venue set to be confirmed – in place ofe the cancelled Finalissima match against European champions Spain in Qatar. Leer más

items.title: Belarus releases 250 political prisoners as part of US deal
items.description: Belarus freed 250 political prisoners in its largest release yet — a move celebrated as a humanitarian breakthrough and tied to the easing of US sanctions. However, many remain behind bars.

items.title: Trump draws parallel between Pearl Harbor and U.S. strikes on Iran in meeting with Japanese PM
items.description: The Japanese attack on ‌the U.S. naval base in Pearl Harbor, Hawaii on Dec. 7, 1941, killed 2,390 Americans, and the U.S. declared war on Japan the next day

items.title: Chuck Norris, US action movie star whose toughness inspired endless memes, dies at 86
items.description: From one studio to another, Chuck Norris turned discipline into legend – a martial arts champion who became the iron-jawed face of "Walker, Texas Ranger" and a larger-than-life cultural myth. He died Thursday at 86.

items.title: News24 | Nigerian president warns of terrorism threat during historic UK state visit
items.description: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu talked of how his country is “challenged by terrorism” as he met Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Thursday as part of his state visit to the UK.

items.title: Venezuela’s 600% inflation undercuts Trump’s boasts of revival

items.description: Anti-apartheid activist, human rights campaigner, and constitutional lawyer, Nicholas “Fink” Haysom, died on Wednesday aged 73.

items.title: I didn't know Epstein was a predator
items.description: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit has said that she wishes she had never met late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein

items.title: News24 | Trump administration mulls large troop deployment to Middle East in new phase of Iran war
items.description: US President Donald Trump’s administration is considering deploying thousands of US troops to reinforce its operation in the Middle East, as the US military prepares for possible next steps in its campaign against Iran, said a US official and three people familiar with the matter.

items.title: News24 | US-Israeli strikes target Iran’s largest gas field, sparking fire at South Pars facility
items.description: US-Israeli strikes hit Iranian facilities at a major Gulf gas field on Wednesday causing a fire, Iranian state television reported.

items.title: EU summit: Hungary holds Ukraine aid ransom over Druzhba oil
items.description: Hungary and Slovakia have said they will not approve a major loan package for Ukraine until oil deliveries from Russia through the Druzhba pipeline are restored. EU leaders failed to sway them in Brussels.

items.title: Australia PM heckled at Sydney mosque Ramadan event
items.description: The government's response to the Israel-Gaza war and rising Islamophobia has angered many Muslim Australians.

items.title: Bayern Munich's Karl and Urbig in latest Germany squad
items.description: Julian Nagelsmann's latest Germany squad includes a host of new names. The 2026 World Cup is less than three months away.

items.title: Alice Springs prison guards go on 12-hour strike over 'unsafe' conditions
items.description: Alice Springs prison guards have taken industrial action, including a 12-hour work stoppage across the town's correctional facilities, over what they say are unsafe conditions.

items.title: Sahel accounts for nearly half of all terror-related deaths: report
items.description: The Sahel region of Africa, which has become the "global epicentre of terrorism," accounted for nearly half of all terrorism-related deaths for the third consecutive year in 2025, the Global Terrorism Report said Thursday.

items.title: Skeleton in Magdeburg Cathedral is almost certainly Otto I
items.description: When the sarcophagus of the former Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great sustained damage and had to be opened, researchers decided to analyze the contents. They're convinced that the remains inside are the real deal.

items.title: News24 | US intelligence contradicts Trump’s Iran war justification, finds no nuclear rebuilding
items.description: US intelligence concluded on Wednesday that Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment capacities that were destroyed in a June 2025 US-Israeli attack, contradicting US President Donald Trump’s justifications for his ongoing war.
